titleStmt.title: SII 40.102: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 102. (A.R. No. B. 82 of 1930.) Tērāla, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a slab set up in front of the Siddhēśvara temple in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1213, Khara, Pushya, ba. 15, Ardhōdaya, Solar eclipse, corresponding to 1292 A.D., January 21, Monday.

Records the gift of tax free land for the maintenance of a lamp in the temple of Siddhēśvara-Mahādēva at Tēṟāla by a certain Rudraya-Peddiṁgāru, for the merit of his brother-in-law Sōmayājula Rudradēva after taking a holy bath in the Rudra-guṇḍa in the temple, on the occasion of solar eclipse.
The inscription mentions another name of the king as Narēndra Chakravarti.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti samadhigata paṁcamahāśabdalaṁ
dāyagajakēsari lāṁchana brāji
sakaladēśādhipatiyunu narēṁdra
cakravartiyununaina śrī kākati
kumāra rudradēva mahārājula
divya śrīpādapadmārādhakuṁḍunu
svasti sakala sēnādhipatiyunu
samuddhata pratipakṣa samuha
vihalōpāya lakṣmīpatiyunu
vikhyāta rājanīti cāṇakyuṁḍunu
jagaddhitamita samadhura vākyuṁḍunu
paracakra pradhānibhayaṁkara ṣāḍguṇya
puṇyuṁḍunu bhaya bhīta ripu nripaśaraṇyu
ṁḍunuṁ bati rājya samuddharaṇuṁḍunu
jagadbharita kīrti vibhūṣaṇuṁḍununaina
śrī sōmayājula rudradēvayaṁgāri
kiṁ buṇyamugānu vāri bāva rudraya
peddiṁgāru svasti śakavarṣaṁbulu 1213
gu khara saṁvatsara puṣya ba 15 Arrdhōdaya
sūryyagrahaṇa kālaṁbuna tēṟāla siddhēśvara
ṁbuna rudraguṁḍaṁbuna tīrrthasnānaṁbu sēsi
siddhēśvara mahādēvara dīpaṁbunaku Ā
tēṟālaṁ gala magamānu suṁkamūnu
talārikamunu marini Oṁṟemi Ā
pēranu Aḍugalēdu sarvamānyamu
dhārāpūrvamu sēsi Ācaṁdrārkamu
nicce dīniki yevvaru yeggudalaṁcinānu
gaṁgakaṟuta vēyu gavilaṁboḍici
na dōṣamunaṁ boṁduru
sadamala dharmmapati vilasaṁnuta kīrti vilāsi
nī manō sadavaritīśamūrti giriśabhaktu
sadācanakramārti damatimādha
kiṁdanayuṁḍu dama
likhi
Digital edition of SII 40.102 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
